name: Tetelcingo Nahuatl

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Tetelcingo Nahuatl (tete1251).

notes: Skeleton. Uto-Aztecan (Nahuan) variety of central Mexico written in a Classical-style Spanish-based orthography. Digraphs tl=[tɬ], tz=[ts], ch=[tʃ], x=[ʃ], cu/hu for labiovelar/[w] and qu=[k] are mapped. The letter c is mapped as [k]; before front vowels it (and z) represents [s], a context this longest-match table does not resolve, so that ambiguity plus the saltillo and any vowel-length contrast are the main PER contributors.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Tetelcingo Nahuatl (tete1251)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
